title: Certara MCP tool-provider manifest
description: Contract a provider package ships at inst/mcp/tools/manifest.json so the Certara.R MCP host can expose its tools. A manifest is either declarative (lists tools + handlers) or builder-based (names an exported function that returns ellmer tools).

schema_version.description: Manifest schema version. The host loads manifests sharing its MAJOR version whose MINOR is not newer.

package.description: Provider package name that exports the handlers/builder.

builder.description: Builder mode: name of an exported function in `package` that returns a list of ellmer tool() objects. Mutually exclusive with `tools`.

tools.description: Declarative mode: one entry per tool. Mutually exclusive with `builder`.

name.description: Tool name exposed to the MCP client.

description.description: LLM-facing operational contract: purpose, when-to-use, when-NOT, return semantics. Keep compact and discriminative (deep docs belong in the KB, not here).

group.description: Optional semantic group label (used by launch profiles and tool retrieval).

gated.description: Surfacing flag only: marks a tool whose handler enforces a confirmation/plan gate. The host uses this to set client approval prompts and to list the tool as gated; it adds no enforcement itself.

precondition.description: What must hold / which tool must run before this one (machine-readable sequencing hint; not enforced).

unlocks.description: Tool names this one typically enables next (sequencing hint surfaced in capabilities and tool retrieval).

idempotent.description: Whether repeated identical calls are safe (no cumulative side effect).

side_effects.description: Whether the tool writes files, launches jobs, or mutates state (vs read-only).

parallel_safe.description: Whether the tool is safe to invoke concurrently with others.

latency_class.description: control_plane = fast in-process; launches_job = spawns an async child and returns; blocking_watch = blocks server-side up to a budget.

risk_tier.description: Coarse risk label for client approval policy and observability.

failure_modes.description: Known failure modes the agent should recognize and how to recover.

handler.description: Name of the exported function in `package` that implements the tool.
